ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the server (e.g., rack, data center) */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the server */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores */,
  "memory_total" REAL /* Total memory capacity of the server */,
  "nvme_total" REAL /* Total NVMe storage capacity of the server */,
  "server_model" TEXT /* Model of the server */,
  "server_vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the server */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date when the server was purchased */,
  "warranty_expiration" TEXT /* Date when the server's warranty expires */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date for the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "nvme_total_util" REAL /* NVMe total utilization percentage */,
  "cpu_util" REAL /* CPU utilization percentage */,
  "memory_used" REAL /* Memory used percentage */,
  "nvme_from_transfer" REAL /* NVMe data transfer rate */,
  "num_workers" REAL /* Number of active workers */,
  "network_in" REAL /* Incoming network traffic rate */,
  "network_out" REAL /* Outgoing network traffic rate */,
  "disk_read" REAL /* Disk read rate */,
  "disk_write" REAL /* Disk write rate */,
  "system_load" REAL /* System load average */,
  "process_count" INTEGER /* Number of running processes */,
  "swap_usage" REAL /* Swap usage percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., CPU high, Memory low) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message of the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., performance metric, external system) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, operator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last activity */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active (e.g., true, false) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the resource */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the resource was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "resource_id" TEXT /* ID of the resource being accessed (e.g., metric_id, alert_id) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to access the system */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource being accessed (e.g., metric, alert, server) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
